title: Select English Works of John Wyclif, Volume III: Miscellaneous Works
summary: CME00031 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected matter in Thomas Arnold's 1871 third volume of Select English Works of John Wyclif. The encoding retains the edition title page, printed pp. xiii–xxiii, all thirty-three numbered selections on pp. 1–523, and the appendix, glossary, and index through p. 545; it omits Arnold's introduction on pp. v–xi and copy-specific physical matter. Arnold's edition ultimately draws on twenty-four identified medieval manuscript codices, sometimes through separately modeled working transcripts or Furnivall's supplied collation rather than Arnold's direct inspection. The record distinguishes base witnesses, material intermediaries, and collated witnesses and preserves Arnold's own attribution doubts, including the demonstrably post-Wyclif no. XXIX. Complete exposed digital objects are cached for the 1871 volume, CCCC MS 296, and Cotton Claudius E III; three Digital Bodleian caches are explicitly partial and do not overlap the cited CME passages. John Rylands English MS 90 and TCD MS 244 have public manuscript viewers but are not locally cached: Manchester prohibits systematic extraction of substantial parts, while TCD's bot mitigation prevented a reliable manifest and physical-coverage audit.
review_notes: Arnold's umbrella Wyclif attribution is a historical edition claim, not a modern whole-volume authorship conclusion.

A base-text chain means Arnold states or directly implies that the printed tract ultimately derives from the named codex; explicit working transcripts sit between edition and codex, while a collation relation records comparison only.
Complete facsimile coverage is always qualified relative to the provider's exposed inventory; absent boards, spine, pastedowns, or unexposed leaves remain disclosed.

Working transcripts and a supplied collation are typed manuscript_witness because schema version 1.0.0 has no intermediary-transcript or collation-artifact entity type; labels and relation chains keep them distinct from medieval codices.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00031
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00031; TEXT/@LANG is enm lat even though the encoded editorial apparatus is modern English.
TEMPHEAD records revision 2016-07-21.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00031
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected portions of Arnold's third volume and marked up with basic TEI semantics.
notes: The public discovery record names Wyclif as author; that inherited field is retained as historical attribution metadata and not treated as a current tract-by-tract conclusion.

entities.label: Thomas Arnold, Select English Works of John Wyclif, volume III (1871)

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Arnold, ed., Select English Works of John Wyclif, vol. III, Miscellaneous Works (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1871), xxiii + 545 pp.
bibliographic.title: Select English Works of John Wyclif. Volume III. Miscellaneous Works

physical_description.extent: title and preliminary matter through p. xxiii; 545 Arabic-numbered pages
notes: The complete series was issued in three volumes in 1869–1871; the present cataloging subject and source are volume III.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 288
entities.description: Arnold's siglum T and base witness for no. I, Super Cantica Sacra.

entities.label: Magdalen College, Oxford, MS 52
entities.description: Arnold's siglum U, consulted for portions of no. I.

holding.notes: The official finding-aid page says the improved Western-manuscript catalog remains in preparation; no exact current public item record or complete facsimile was found.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 789
entities.description: Arnold's siglum V and base witness for nos. II, III, and V.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 647
entities.description: Arnold's siglum W and principal source for nos. IX, XIX, XX, XXIV, XXV, XXXI, and XXXII.

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 296
entities.description: Arnold's siglum X and the most extensively used base witness in volume III, supplying or supporting nos. XIII, XIV, XV, XVII, XVIII, XXII, XXIV, XXVIII, and XXXIII.

notes: Parker describes a multi-author collection once wholly attributed to Wyclif.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, MS 408
entities.description: Arnold's siglum Y, base witness for nos. VI–VIII and a parallel witness for nos. III and V.

holding.notes: The holding's medieval manuscripts are available on commercial microfilm; no exact current public complete digital object was found.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 274
entities.description: Arnold's siglum Z and base witness for no. XXVII.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, MS 244
entities.description: A 15th-century Wycliffite compilation used as base for nos. XII and XXI and as a parallel or incomplete witness for several CCCC 296 texts.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 273
entities.description: Arnold's siglum BB, unique base witness for no. XXIX and a parallel witness for no. IX.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, MS 245
entities.description: A Wycliffite compilation used as base for no. XXVI and collated for nos. III, V, IX, X, XXIII, XXVII, and XXVIII.

holding.notes: No exact current public item record or complete digital object was found; the old-to-current pressmark crosswalk is supported by specialist scholarship.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2398
entities.description: Arnold's siglum DD; no. IV occupies ff. 166v–174r and reached Arnold through a transcript made by the person Arnold names only as Mr Brock.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.value: early first quarter of the 15th century

notes: The no. IV Pater Noster is ff. 166v–174r; the same codex also contains a Short Rule at ff. 188v–190v, but Arnold bases no. XV elsewhere.

entities.label: Mr Brock's intermediary transcript of the Pater Noster in Harley MS 2398
entities.description: The otherwise untraced working transcript from which Arnold printed no. IV. Arnold explicitly says that he did not inspect Harley MS 2398 and identifies the transcriber only as Mr Brock.
notes: Typed as a manuscript witness because the schema has no dedicated intermediary-transcript entity type; this modern editorial transcript is not the medieval Harley codex.

entities.label: Unnamed transcript of Lambeth Palace Library MS 408 for nos. VI–VIII
entities.description: The working transcript on which Arnold says he founded nos. VI–VIII; he explicitly states that he did not see Lambeth MS 408.

entities.label: W. Aldis Wright-revised transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XIV
entities.description: A careful working transcript of no. XIV from CCCC MS 296, revised against the codex by W. Aldis Wright before Arnold used it.

entities.label: Mr T. French's transcript of TCD MS 244 for no. XXI
entities.description: The careful transcript made for the Oxford University Press Delegates from TCD MS 244 (then C.III.12) and used by Arnold for no. XXI.

entities.label: Cambridge transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XXII
entities.description: The working transcript on which Arnold based no. XXII, examined against CCCC MS 296 by W. Aldis Wright before it was sent to him.

entities.label: Older transcript of the no. XXII tract made for John Lewis
entities.description: A roughly eighteenth-century transcript of the CCCC MS 296 tract made for John Lewis, reported by Arnold among the Rawlinsonian manuscripts and used to compare his Cambridge transcript.
notes: Arnold reports its historical Bodleian location but supplies no shelfmark; a current exact catalog record has not been established.

entities.label: Mr T. French's transcript of TCD MS 245 for no. XXVI
entities.description: The transcript made for the Oxford University Press Delegates from TCD MS 245 (then C.V.6) and used by Arnold for no. XXVI.

entities.label: Unnamed transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XXVIII
entities.description: The working transcript from CCCC MS 296 on which Arnold says he founded no. XXVIII.

entities.label: Unnamed transcript of MS Bodley 788 for no. XXIII
entities.description: The working transcript of the no. XXIII tract in MS Bodley 788 made for Arnold's edition.

entities.label: Frederick Furnivall's collation of the Corser manuscript against Harley MS 2398
entities.description: The working collation supplied to Arnold for no. IV, comparing the manuscript now John Rylands English MS 90 with Harley MS 2398.

entities.label: John Rylands Library, English MS 90
entities.description: The manuscript Arnold knew as Mr Corser's newly discovered codex. Its Pater Noster on ff. 63r–65v was collated by Furnivall against Harley MS 2398 for no. IV.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.value: end of the 14th or beginning of the 15th century

physical_description.format: Codex containing the Pricke of Conscience and a Pater Noster exposition

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 174
entities.description: Arnold's siglum FF and base witness for no. XV; it also preserves portions of no. I.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2385
entities.description: Arnold's siglum GG and a parallel witness for nos. III, V, and XXVII.

notes: Relevant first-part texts include a Pater Noster, Seven Heresies, and Ave Maria on ff. 2r–5v.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius C VII
entities.description: Arnold's siglum HH, a Knighton Chronicle witness consulted for the eucharistic material in nos. XXX–XXXI; the relevant modern comparison locus is ff. 183v–184v.

notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Claudius E III
entities.description: Arnold's siglum II, another Knighton Chronicle witness for nos. XXX–XXXI; the relevant modern comparison locus is ff. 275v–276r.

notes: The Knighton Chronicle component is ff. 169r–307r; the relevant eucharistic variants are ff. 275v–276r.

entities.label: New College, Oxford, MS 95
entities.description: A Wycliffite sermon compilation serving as base for nos. X, XI, and XVI and as a partial comparison witness for nos. I and XXXII.

holding.notes: The official narrative collection page confirms a collection of Wycliffite sermons; no exact item record or complete public facsimile was found.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 788
entities.description: Arnold's stated base witness for no. XXIII, The Church and Her Members.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 448
entities.description: A partial comparison witness for no. I.

entities.label: University College, Oxford, MS 56
entities.description: A comparison witness for the Rolle-derived portion of no. I.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 286
entities.description: A comparison witness for the first seven canticle commentaries in no. I.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 524
entities.description: A comparison witness for an alternate form of no. II.

entities.label: University College, Oxford, MS 97
entities.description: A comparison witness for an alternate form of no. II.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 258
entities.description: A comparison witness for the twelfth canticle commentary in no. I.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Arnold's 1871 volume III
entities.description: A 578-page PDF derived from the University of Michigan copy, covering the combined exterior boards and spine, inside boards, flyleaves, library marks, half-title and title matter, all printed pages, terminal marks, and rear exterior.

entities.label: Parker Library complete exposed facsimile of CCCC MS 296
entities.description: A 612-canvas IIIF object: paired NC and TC captures for 306 manuscript surfaces. It is complete relative to the published inventory but does not expose boards, spine, or separately identified pastedowns.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of MS Bodley 788
entities.description: Ten published canvases covering fols. 1r–5v only. No. XXIII occurs near the end of the manuscript, so the selected images have zero verified overlap with the CME source passage.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of MS Laud Misc. 448
entities.description: Two published canvases, fols. 1v and 41r. They do not provide verified coverage of the specific no. I portions Arnold compared.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image of MS Laud Misc. 286
entities.description: One published canvas, fol. 1r, showing the Psalter incipit rather than the later canticle material Arnold compared for no. I.

entities.label: British Library complete exposed facsimile of Cotton Claudius E III
entities.description: A 727-canvas IIIF object from front cover through spine, including flyleaves, blanks, all 356 folios, covers, and other exposed physical surfaces. The CME comparison passage is ff. 275v–276r, canvases 558–559.

entities.label: Manchester complete exposed facsimile of John Rylands English MS 90
entities.description: A 152-canvas public IIIF object covering upper and lower covers, spine, inside covers, flyleaves, all leaves, and color charts. The no. IV Pater Noster is ff. 63r–65v, canvases 135–140.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin digital object for MS 244
entities.description: The official digitised-manuscript list links a public Digital Collections object for MS 244. Current bot mitigation prevented retrieval of the manifest, canvas count, folio mapping, and exact physical-object coverage, so completeness is not asserted.
entities.reproduction_format: Public web viewer / IIIF reported by platform

rights.component: U-M CME00031 text and presentation as offered
rights.basis: The U-M item presents the source as public-domain material and links the No Copyright – United States statement.
notes: This provider assertion does not merge rights in medieval texts, Arnold's print edition, U-M encoding, manuscript photography, or repository copy into one layer.

rights.component: Arnold's 1871 editorial text, arrangement, notes, and reference matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1871, and the U-M derived item identifies its source material as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Medieval texts reproduced within the edition
rights.basis: The underlying texts are medieval; this assessment is separate from Arnold's editorial layer and from modern digital reproductions.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF and digital reproduction
rights.basis: The underlying 1871 edition is public domain and the PDF is openly downloadable, but current provider metadata supplies no explicit rights field or reusable-file license for the scan derivative.
notes: Public download access is not treated as an express waiver of all rights in the derivative.

rights.component: Parker Library manuscript images and metadata
rights.basis: The Parker object and IIIF manifest assign Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 to the digital content.
notes: The cache is for noncommercial research and does not imply commercial reuse permission.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian selected images and metadata
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian attributes the item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 and supplies a separate commercial-permission route.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian selected images and metadata
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian attributes the item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 and supplies a separate commercial-permission route.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian selected image and metadata
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian attributes the item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 and supplies a separate commercial-permission route.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photography and IIIF delivery
rights.basis: The manifest states that the content is public domain in most countries other than the UK and links British Library usage terms; UK publication or redistribution permission is therefore not inferred.
notes: The underlying medieval text is distinct from the modern photography and delivery layer.

rights.component: Manchester manuscript photography, metadata, and delivery
rights.basis: The manifest reserves rights in the zooming image and describes download images and metadata as CC BY-NC 4.0, while current site terms prohibit systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts.
notes: Because the general extraction prohibition materially limits a whole-object cache, no bulk local copy is retained without written authorization.

rights.component: TCD manuscript photography and Digital Collections delivery
rights.basis: TCD's current open-licensing policy generally places digital surrogates of out-of-copyright collection items under CC BY 4.0 but identifies exceptions and asks users to clarify the license or publication permission; the exact MS 244 item license could not be inspected through current bot mitigation.
notes: Do not treat the general policy as an item-level license confirmation until the MS 244 object metadata is inspected or TCD responds.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Shirley's catalog of sixty-five English works attributed to Wyclif
editorial_practices.representation: Thirty-two complete works and part of another, plus editorial framing, arranged in three thematic parts
editorial_practices.description: Arnold describes his selection as intended to represent exegetical, didactic, controversial, and documentary writing while excluding some previously printed, repetitive, unsuitable, or inauthentic material.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple manuscript witnesses, partial copies, transcripts, and Chronicle witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Selected base texts with variant comparison described in headnotes and notes
editorial_practices.description: Arnold based texts on principal witnesses and collated alternatives, sometimes through Brock's or French's transcripts, Wright-reviewed transcripts, unnamed working transcripts, Furnivall's supplied collation, or an older Rawlinsonian transcript. The lineage relations preserve these different modes rather than flattening every cited codex into a direct material source.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page, pp. xiii–xxiii, and pp. 1–545 encoded; introduction pp. v–xi and physical copy matter omitted
editorial_practices.description: Direct page-by-page comparison establishes the encoded boundary. The U-M collection page describes manual keying, proofreading, and basic TEI semantics but does not give an item-level rationale for this selection.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for CME00031
evidence.summary: Establishes repository identity, compact source metadata, text language attribute, and exact encoded content.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual inspection of CME00031 XML against Arnold's edition
evidence.locator: title page; DIV1/DIV2/DIV3 structure; PB milestones; thirty-three numbered texts; appendix, glossary, and index
evidence.summary: Verifies identifiers, edition-level boundary, exact source selection, 11 DIV1, 38 DIV2, 219 DIV3, 1,201 P, 1,380 NOTE, 561 PB, 6 LG, and 41 L elements. Only one LG and three L elements belong directly to a medieval selection; the other five LG and 38 L elements are within editorial NOTE or HEADNOTE. It also verifies irregular division structure under nos. VI–VIII and X.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME00031
evidence.summary: Confirms stable item identity, edition title and publication information, public access, inherited Wyclif author field, and No Copyright – United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse
evidence.summary: Describes the 2016–2018 expansion as manual keying from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and editing and basic TEI encoding.

evidence.citation: Thomas Arnold, ed., Select English Works of John Wyclif, vol. III (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1871), University of Michigan/Internet Archive copy
evidence.locator: title; introduction pp. v–xi; manuscript list pp. xiii–xvi; work list and contents pp. xvii–xxiii; headnotes and texts pp. 1–523; appendix pp. 525–529; glossary pp. 530–538; index pp. 539–545
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the volume III date and extent, selection and attribution cautions, manuscript sigla and headnotes, Brock and Furnivall's roles, base and collateral witnesses, content boundaries, and complete physical scan coverage.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for afb3713.0003.001.umich.edu
evidence.summary: Confirms item and source-file identity, public PDF download, 40,179,995-byte derivative, and absence of an explicit rights or license field. Its 1869 date is series-level and less precise than the inspected volume III title page dated 1871.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record for Select English Works of John Wyclif
evidence.summary: Confirms the three-volume Oxford Clarendon Press edition, 1869–1871 series span, Arnold's editorial responsibility, and full-view HathiTrust route for volume III.

evidence.citation: Parker Library on the Web, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 296
evidence.locator: catalog contents; IIIF canvases 1–612; manuscript pp. 39–65, 144–160, 221–234, and 239–298
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, date, multi-author Wycliffite context, exact tract page ranges, 612-canvas paired-capture inventory, public access, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts and Archives at Oxford University records for Arnold's Bodleian and University College witnesses
evidence.summary: Current official records confirm exact identities, dates, languages and contents where cataloged, current digital-image extent for three selected-image objects, and the absence of complete public facsimiles for the remaining listed witnesses.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use
evidence.summary: Confirms CC BY-NC 4.0 for attributed Digital Bodleian content and the separate permission route for commercial use.

evidence.citation: Magdalen College Old Library finding aids and access pages
evidence.summary: States that the improved Western-manuscript catalog is in preparation and provides the advance-appointment, enquiry, and imaging route; no exact public MS 52 record or complete digital object was found.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Lambeth Palace: The Mediaeval Manuscripts (1932), with the current World Microfilms series
evidence.summary: Provides the standard catalog context and confirms that the medieval manuscripts, including MS 408, are available on microfilm; no exact public complete digital object or open reuse license was found.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Dublin, Digitised Manuscripts
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark MS 244, 15th-century Wycliffite miscellaneous-tract description, public Digital Collections object, and ongoing manuscript digitization. MS 245 is absent from the current list.

evidence.citation: The Library of Trinity College Dublin, Rights Information
evidence.summary: Explains the conditional CC BY 4.0 policy for digital surrogates of out-of-copyright items, exceptions, and the route to clarify item-level licensing or publication permission.

evidence.citation: Estelle V. Stubbs, 'Seeking Scribal Communities in Medieval London,' in Scribal Cultures in Late Medieval England (2022), with the Cambridge bibliography of scholarship on TCD MS 245
evidence.summary: Supports the historical-to-current pressmark crosswalk C.III.12 = TCD MS 244 and C.V.6 = TCD MS 245 and the Wycliffite compilation context; Arnold remains the direct evidence for how he used each codex.
notes: The exact current TCD MS 245 catalog record remains unavailable publicly and is retained as an open access question.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 2398
evidence.summary: Confirms exact shelfmark, early-15th-century date and southwestern English context, 193-folio extent, and no. IV Pater Noster on ff. 166v–174r; no complete public images are linked.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 2385
evidence.summary: Confirms the composite codex, 70-folio extent, and relevant Pater Noster, Seven Heresies, and Ave Maria on ff. 2r–5v; no complete public images are linked.

evidence.citation: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius C VII
evidence.summary: Confirms the late-14th-century Knighton Chronicle witness, Leicester Abbey origin, 240-folio extent, and 1731 fire damage; no complete public images are linked.

evidence.citation: British Library, Cotton MS Claudius E III catalog and complete IIIF object
evidence.locator: 727 canvases; Knighton component ff. 169r–307r; relevant ff. 275v–276r = canvases 558–559
evidence.summary: Confirms the whole codex and Knighton component identities, dates and extents, full exposed-object canvas coverage, exact CME passage mapping, and provider rights statement.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Digital Collections, John Rylands English MS 90
evidence.locator: Pater Noster ff. 63r–65v; IIIF canvases 135–140 of 152
evidence.summary: Identifies Arnold's Corser manuscript, confirms shelfmark, date, material, extent, contents and provenance, and exposes a complete 152-canvas public viewer from upper through lower cover.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Digital Collections, Terms and Conditions
evidence.summary: Permits limited personal and noncommercial extracts but prohibits systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts and creation of another database.

evidence.citation: New College, Oxford, Reformation collection page and rare-manuscript access route
evidence.summary: Confirms New College MS 95 as a collection of Wycliffite sermons and provides the institutional enquiry route; no complete public facsimile was found.

evidence.citation: Margaret Aston, 'Wyclif and the Vernacular,' Studies in Church History Subsidia 5 (1987), 281–330
evidence.locator: edition of the eucharistic confessions and note 1
evidence.summary: Supports cautious vernacular attribution and supplies exact comparison loci for the eucharistic texts: Bodley 647 f. 70r–v, Cotton Tiberius C VII ff. 183v–184v, and Cotton Claudius E III ff. 275v–276r.

open_questions.question: What current specialist attribution, date, and textual-layer model applies to each numbered selection and to the Rolle/Wycliffite layers of no. I?
next_steps: Review each tract against Anne Hudson's bibliography and later critical editions; retain Arnold's umbrella attribution only as disputed historical evidence until that review is complete.

open_questions.question: Is Arnold's front-list assignment of no. XXIII to CCCC MS 296 a typographical error for no. XXVIII?
next_steps: Compare modern CCCC 296 tract handlists with Arnold's no. XXIII and XXVIII headnotes; preserve Bodley 788 as the directly stated base for XXIII meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Which of the working transcripts and Furnivall collation used for Arnold's volume survive, and where are they now held?
next_steps: Trace the Brock, Lambeth, Wright-revised, French, Cambridge, Lewis/Rawlinson, Bodley 788, CCCC 296, and Furnivall materials through Oxford University Press Delegate records, Arnold papers, collaborator correspondence, and repository catalogs; preserve surname- or initials-only identities unless documentary evidence resolves them.

open_questions.question: What are TCD MS 244's canvas inventory, physical-object coverage, exact CME tract loci, and item-level reuse terms?
next_steps: Use an interactive browser or ask TCD Digital Collections for the IIIF manifest, canvas-to-folio map, statement about boards/flyleaves/blanks/exteriors, and exact item license; cache only if the verified terms permit whole-object retrieval.
notes: Current reCAPTCHA bot mitigation blocked unattended retrieval; failed automation is not treated as evidence that the object is unavailable to users. No repository enquiry has yet been sent.

open_questions.question: Can authorized complete digital copies be obtained for the manuscript witnesses that lack a complete public image object, especially Bodley 288, Bodley 789, Bodley 647, Lambeth 408, TCD MS 245, Harley 2398, Harley 2385, Cotton Tiberius C VII, New College MS 95, and Magdalen MS 52?
next_steps: Use the exact catalog and repository enquiry routes to request whole-object availability, image counts, coverage of covers/spine/pastedowns/flyleaves/blanks, cost, and reuse terms; do not treat selected images or commercial microfilm as complete without an inventory audit.
notes: No repository enquiry has yet been sent.

open_questions.question: Will Manchester authorize a whole-object research cache or supply an institutionally approved complete reproduction of John Rylands English MS 90?
next_steps: Request written clarification or an authorized bundle through Manchester's rights/reproduction route; do not bulk-extract the 152-canvas public object under current systematic-extraction terms.
notes: No permission enquiry has yet been sent.

open_questions.question: What U-M production record explains retention of the title page and pp. xiii–545 while omitting Arnold's introduction on pp. v–xi?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level selection, keying, proofreading, and source-copy notes while preserving the directly observed mapping.
